tasks.task: Take a deep breath and perform an initial scan to identify the contract type: content licensing agreement, talent/performance agreement, music synchronization license, distribution agreement, digital rights/UGC license, or hybrid/other.
Extract the document header, title, and any template or disclaimer notices.
Identify all contracting parties, their defined short names (e.g., "Licensor", "Performer"), organizational types, jurisdictions, and principal office locations.
Extract all recitals (WHEREAS clauses) establishing the purpose and background of the agreement.

Identify and catalog every defined term in the agreement. Defined terms are typically capitalized and introduced with quotation marks or parenthetical definitions. Extract each term and its full definition verbatim.

Extract the complete grant of rights, including: Nature of the grant (exclusive, non-exclusive, non-transferable) Specific rights granted (reproduce, distribute, perform, display, synchronize, make available, exploit) Licensed media and platforms with all enumerated categories Territory specifications Sublicensing and assignment restrictions Reservation of rights language Holdback provisions and exclusivity restrictions

Extract all financial and compensation terms, including: Fixed fees, license fees, and synchronization fees with amounts or blank indicators Revenue share percentages and the definition of the revenue base (Net Revenue, Gross Receipts, Net Profits) Minimum guarantees and recoupment mechanics Residuals and royalty structures including guild references Distribution fees and commission rates Recoupable expense categories Payment schedules, thresholds, and reporting periods Most-favored-nations provisions Per diem, travel, and production allowances Contingent compensation triggers

Extract term and termination provisions, including: Initial term duration and commencement trigger Renewal mechanics, notice periods, and automatic renewal conditions Termination triggers (breach, force majeure, incapacity, abandonment) Cure periods and notice requirements Post-termination obligations (cease exploitation, return/destroy materials, final accounting) Reversion of rights provisions Sell-off periods for existing commitments Survival clauses identifying which provisions outlast termination

Extract all obligation and restriction provisions, including: Delivery requirements and technical specifications Exclusivity periods and appearance/alteration restrictions Morals clauses Approval and consultation rights (marketing, key art, credit, trailers) Reporting, accounting, and audit rights with frequency and notice periods Content standards and community guidelines compliance DMCA and takedown compliance obligations Content moderation rights and appeal processes Data portability and analytics access provisions Publicity and promotional obligations
Extract credit and attribution provisions, including screen credit specifications, paid advertising obligations, music credit requirements, and credit remedy limitations.

Extract all legal protection provisions: Representations and warranties by each party Indemnification obligations (mutual or asymmetric) Limitation of liability and damage exclusions Governing law and jurisdiction Dispute resolution mechanism (arbitration, mediation, litigation) Force majeure provisions with enumerated triggering events Entire agreement, amendment, and waiver clauses
Identify any AI/digital likeness provisions, including consent requirements, additional compensation triggers, and scope limitations.
Extract all references to exhibits, schedules, and attachments, noting their described contents even if the exhibits themselves are not present in the image.
Note all signature blocks, including party names, signatory titles, and date fields.
If no media rights contract content is found, respond with the not_found_response as specified in the verification process.
